thesis: Biogen has meaningful marketed and pipeline optionality, but current product cash-flow schedules, indication-level probabilities and the post-Apellis closing balance sheet are insufficient to reproduce the wide component ranges.

explanation_contracts.mechanism: Biogen has meaningful marketed and pipeline optionality, but current product cash-flow schedules, indication-level probabilities and the post-Apellis closing balance sheet are insufficient to reproduce the wide component ranges.
explanation_contracts.distinguishing_test: What product-level net cash-flow and patent schedule reproduces legacy and growth values?
explanation_contracts.counter_explanation: The model may count a portfolio narrative as diversified optionality even though clinical, reimbursement, patent and financing outcomes are correlated and the closing claims are not yet known.
explanation_contracts.falsifier: Growth products fail to offset legacy erosion
explanation_contracts.valuation_link: Primary method: product-level DCF plus non-overlapping milestone probability trees, less senior claims and correlated-downside reserve; do not accept the current range until its component bridges reproduce.

open_questions: What product-level net cash-flow and patent schedule reproduces legacy and growth values?
What independent clinical base rates, dependencies, remaining R&D and launch capital reproduce each event branch?
What post-close debt, CVR liability, integration cost and required liquidity reconcile senior claims?
